rules.description: Prepayment penalty on floating rate MSME loans is prohibited
rules.source: RBI Master Direction – Interest Rate on Advances, 2016 (Updated 2022); RBI/2019-20/170
rules.plain_rule: RBI explicitly prohibits lenders from charging any prepayment penalty on MSME loans carrying floating interest rates. Any such charge is a direct violation and is unenforceable.

rules.description: Penal charges must not be compounded; they cannot be added to principal for further interest levy
rules.source: RBI Circular on Penal Charges in Loan Accounts – RBI/2023-24/53, August 18, 2023
rules.plain_rule: RBI's August 2023 circular mandates that penal charges be levied as a flat fee and must not be compounded or capitalised into the principal. Compounding penalties on top of penalties is explicitly prohibited.

rules.description: Penal interest rate for default should not be excessive; any rate beyond 2% per month over the contracted rate is considered disproportionate
rules.source: RBI Circular on Penal Charges in Loan Accounts – RBI/2023-24/53; RBI Fair Practices Code
rules.plain_rule: RBI requires penal charges to be reasonable and proportionate. A penal interest rate exceeding 24% per annum (2% per month) compounded on the contracted rate is typically flagged as excessive and may be challenged.

rules.description: All charges and fees must be disclosed upfront in a Key Facts Statement (KFS) before loan sanction
rules.source: RBI Circular on Key Facts Statement for Loans and Advances – RBI/2023-24/75, April 15, 2024
trigger_keywords: as amended from time to time
rules.plain_rule: Effective April 2024, RBI mandates all lenders to provide a Key Facts Statement (KFS) disclosing every charge, fee, and cost before disbursement. Vague references to future or discretionary charges are a direct violation of this circular.

rules.description: Processing fee for MSME loans must be disclosed upfront, non-refundable portion must be stated, and total cost must appear in KFS
rules.source: RBI Fair Practices Code for Lenders; RBI/2023-24/75 (KFS Mandate)
rules.plain_rule: Processing fees must be disclosed before loan sanction as part of the Key Facts Statement. Any undisclosed processing fee, or one that is changed after application, violates RBI fair practices.

rules.description: Insurance products cannot be bundled mandatorily with loans; premiums must be disclosed and borrower must have choice of insurer
rules.source: RBI Circular on Bundling of Financial Products – RBI/2012-13/202; IRDAI Guidelines
rules.plain_rule: Banks cannot force borrowers to purchase insurance as a condition for the loan or bundle it without disclosure. If insurance is required, the borrower must be free to choose the insurer and the exact premium must be stated upfront.

rules.description: All new floating rate loans to MSMEs must be linked to an External Benchmark Rate (repo rate or T-bill) and reset at least every 3 months
rules.source: RBI Circular on External Benchmark Based Lending Rate – RBI/2019-20/142, September 4, 2019
rules.plain_rule: Since October 1, 2019, RBI requires all new floating rate loans to MSMEs to be linked to an external benchmark such as the RBI Repo Rate. Loans pegged only to an internal benchmark are non-compliant with this mandatory directive.

rules.description: Any change in the interest rate must be communicated to the borrower with adequate prior notice; unilateral silent changes are prohibited
rules.source: RBI Fair Practices Code for Lenders, 2003 (Updated 2012); Consumer Protection Act 2019
trigger_keywords: lender may at any time change
rules.plain_rule: RBI's Fair Practices Code requires banks to inform borrowers of any interest rate changes in advance before they take effect. A clause allowing silent, unilateral rate revisions without notice is a violation of borrower rights.

rules.description: MSME loans up to Rs. 2 crore are eligible for collateral-free lending under CGTMSE; demanding collateral for such loans may be non-compliant
rules.source: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) Master Circular 2023
rules.plain_rule: Under the CGTMSE scheme, loans to micro and small enterprises up to ₹2 crore should be extended without collateral or third-party guarantees. Demanding collateral for covered loans without justification may violate scheme norms.

rules.description: Lenders cannot exercise right of set-off over borrower deposits without providing prior notice; silent set-off is prohibited
rules.source: RBI Fair Practices Code; Banking Ombudsman Scheme 2022 – Ground (b)(xiv)
rules.plain_rule: Banks must provide prior notice before invoking right of set-off against a borrower's deposits. Exercising lien without notice is a violation of fair practices and is an explicit ground for Banking Ombudsman complaints under the 2022 scheme.

rules.description: Blanket lien or charge over all assets of the borrower (beyond agreed collateral) constitutes collateral overreach
rules.source: RBI Guidelines on Fair Practices Code; SARFAESI Act 2002
rules.plain_rule: Collateral must be restricted to assets specifically agreed upon in the sanction letter. A blanket charge over all present and future assets of the borrower goes beyond the agreed security and constitutes overreach.

rules.description: Borrowers cannot be contractually forced to waive their right to approach the Banking Ombudsman, consumer courts, or civil courts
rules.source: Banking Ombudsman Scheme 2022; Consumer Protection Act 2019; RBI Fair Practices Code
rules.plain_rule: Borrowers have an inalienable statutory right to approach the Banking Ombudsman, consumer courts, and civil courts in India. Any contractual waiver of these rights is legally void and unenforceable.

rules.description: Arbitration clauses must allow for mutual appointment of the arbitrator; bank-only appointment creates bias and may be invalid
rules.source: Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996 (Amended 2019) – Section 11; RBI Guidelines
rules.plain_rule: Under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996 (as amended), the arbitrator must be mutually agreed upon. Clauses giving the bank sole right to appoint the arbitrator create an inherently biased process and have been struck down by Indian courts.

rules.description: Repayment structure with a large lump-sum (balloon) payment at maturity must be clearly disclosed and justified by borrower cash flows
rules.source: RBI Master Direction on Priority Sector Lending 2020; RBI Fair Practices Code
rules.plain_rule: Balloon payment structures must be explicitly disclosed in the Key Facts Statement and must be justified by the borrower's cash flow profile. Undisclosed or inappropriate balloon structures can trap borrowers in unmanageable debt at maturity.

rules.description: Acceleration of the entire loan outstanding for minor or first-time defaults without adequate notice period is an unfair practice
rules.source: RBI Fair Practices Code for Lenders, 2003; Indian Contract Act 1872 Section 74
trigger_keywords: entire dues become payable on default
rules.plain_rule: Declaring the entire loan immediately repayable upon a minor or first default without a notice and cure period is considered an unfair and disproportionate practice under RBI's Fair Practices Code.

rules.description: Lenders must provide at least 3 months’ notice before increasing margin or spread on MSME loans; rate reset period for EBLR-linked loans is 3 months
rules.source: RBI/2019-20/142 on EBLR; RBI Fair Practices Code – Para 3(b)
rules.plain_rule: For EBLR-linked loans, the spread (margin above the benchmark) can only be revised upward due to a material change in credit risk and must be reset at intervals not less than 3 months. Arbitrary revision of spreads is prohibited.
